mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount rate on equities declines and risk appetite improves, so S&P 500 forward returns over the next month are positive. The signal is invalid if rate cuts occur alongside rising inflation expectations proxies or if equities fail to rally after sustained easing.
failure_condition: Reject if negative or flat 20-day forward returns dominate after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal's hit rate falls below 50% in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% indicates restrictive policy that tightens financial conditions and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest once rates are clearly above neutral and recession risk is elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return in the trigger state is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening income growth and deteriorating demand, which should weigh on corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when UNRATE rises quickly rather than being merely elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-surge triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than half the time, or if the sign of the average forward return is positive across triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A combined improvement in retail sales and industrial production indicates broad-based real activity momentum, supporting earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is not decomposable to either series alone because the signal requires simultaneous demand and supply confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-positive RSAFS and INDPRO state does not outperform the unconditional benchmark, or if either series alone explains the same effect without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are contracting sharply, the signal identifies a demand-side slump that usually precedes weaker corporate sales and lower equity prices over the next quarter. A decline in HOUST is especially bearish when it persists for several months.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns after three-month housing declines are not negative on average, or if the trigger state fails to underperform the unconditional distribution.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in INDPRO and PAYEMS indicates improving production capacity and payroll formation, which should lift forward S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through stronger earnings breadth. The interaction matters because output gains without payroll gains do not confirm broad recovery.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with concurrent rises in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not produce positive 60-day returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, rising GDPC1 confirms a rebound in aggregate demand and should support positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. The signal should work when real GDP is accelerating from a weak base and fail when the economy is already at a mature expansion peak.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDP acceleration does not correspond to positive 60-day forward returns, or if the sign weakens materially outside recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE is rising, labor market deterioration is broad enough to imply falling household income and weaker earnings, so S&P 500 returns over the next month should be negative. The joint condition is needed because either indicator alone can be noisy.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-deterioration trigger is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances, or if the average forward return is not below zero.
